rosters.notes: Direct beneficiaries of AI data-centre power demand: VRT (Vertiv — cooling and power infrastructure), ETN (Eaton — power management systems), PWR (Quanta Services — grid and electrical engineering), CEG (Constellation Energy — nuclear power supply, hyperscaler PPAs), GEV (GE Vernova — grid solutions, power conversion, gas/nuclear at scale; $259.7B FMP-verified), VST (Vistra Corp — second-largest US nuclear fleet, Microsoft PPA signed; $53.9B FMP-verified). XLU as sector ETF proxy.

AI semiconductor infrastructure: NVDA (GPU accelerators), TSM (advanced foundry, N3/N2 capacity), AVGO (networking ASICs, AI cluster interconnects), AMD (GPU competition, CPU/EPYC), ASML (EUV lithography equipment), LRCX (Lam Research — etch/deposition equipment), KLAC (KLA — yield management and inspection), MRVL (Marvell Technology — custom AI ASICs for hyperscalers including Amazon Trainium and Google TPU connectivity; $180.1B FMP-verified). SMH as sector ETF proxy.

Net-interest-margin beneficiaries when rates rising and credit contained: JPM (largest US bank), BAC (most rate-sensitive mega bank), WFC (pure-play NIM expansion), GS (capital markets + NIM), C (Citigroup — global systemically important bank, large US deposit base; $216.1B FMP-verified). XLF as diversified financial sector proxy. Route bias swing_only.

Oil supply shock beneficiaries: XOM (integrated oil, largest US energy), CVX (integrated oil, Permian exposure), OXY (pure-play E&P, high oil beta), SLB (oilfield services, rig count beneficiary), COP (ConocoPhillips — largest pure-play US E&P by market cap, no refining drag, Permian and LNG exposure; $139.2B FMP-verified). XLE as energy sector ETF proxy.

Geopolitical risk prime contractors: LMT (Lockheed Martin — F-35, missile systems), NOC (Northrop Grumman — stealth bombers, space), RTX (missiles, engines, radar), GD (General Dynamics — combat vehicles, submarines), LHX (L3Harris Technologies — electronic warfare, ISR systems, tactical communications, space sensors; $58.5B FMP-verified). ITA as aerospace/defence ETF proxy.

High-quality compounders with strong free cash flow, minimal debt dependency, and resilient earnings: MSFT (software monopoly, cloud), AAPL (brand loyalty, services, large cash), COST (resilient consumer demand), BRK.B (diversified quality with large cash), V (Visa — asset-light payments network, ~50%+ FCF margins, durable moat; $631.4B FMP-verified), MA (Mastercard — identical payments network model, ~45%+ FCF margins; $436.9B FMP-verified). QUAL ETF as sector proxy.

Defensive quality names with low volatility and resilient demand: COST (essential retail), JNJ (healthcare, stable cash flows), PG (consumer staples, pricing power), KO (Coca-Cola), PEP (PepsiCo), WMT (Walmart — dominant essential retail franchise, strongest defensive retail globally; $927.6B FMP-verified), CL (Colgate-Palmolive — oral care and personal hygiene, exceptional pricing power; $72.3B FMP-verified), ABT (Abbott Laboratories — diagnostics, medical devices, nutrition, recurring cash flows; $149.4B FMP-verified). ETF proxies: XLP, XLV, SPLV.

IWM (iShares Russell 2000 ETF) as small-cap proxy. Used for headwind monitoring only under credit stress — watchlist-only, non-executable. No core symbols — individual small-cap selection requires additional intelligence layer not yet built.

Memory and storage names with direct AI data-centre demand exposure. MU (Micron — only US-listed pure-play HBM/DRAM manufacturer; NOT in semiconductors roster). SNDK (Sandisk Corporation — re-listed independent NAND flash business spun out from Western Digital Feb 2024). WDC (Western Digital — enterprise HDD and storage post-SNDK spinout). STX (Seagate Technology — enterprise HDD). NTAP (NetApp — hybrid cloud and data-centric storage infrastructure, ONTAP software and all-flash FAS arrays deployed in AI data-centre storage tiers; $34.6B FMP-verified).

Approved AI compute infrastructure names. IREN (approved with caution — power-constrained neocloud, speculative risk). SMCI (Super Micro Computer — high-density server and rack-scale AI solutions). DELL (Dell Technologies — server and storage infrastructure for enterprise AI). CRWV (CoreWeave — GPU-native hyperscaler purpose-built for AI training and inference, bare-metal GPU clusters for enterprise GenAI; $59.5B FMP-verified). Approval does not imply all neocloud or bitcoin-miner names are approved.

SaaS and enterprise cloud platforms benefiting from falling yields and risk-on rotation. CRM (Salesforce), NOW (ServiceNow), ADBE (Adobe), DDOG (Datadog), SNOW (Snowflake — pure-play cloud data platform, high-beta SaaS name, direct enterprise cloud exposure with durable growth; $88.1B FMP-verified). IGV as iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF proxy. MSFT covered separately in quality_cash_flow.

Cybersecurity platform vendors benefiting from AI-driven expansion of attack surface and enterprise security spend. CRWD (CrowdStrike), PANW (Palo Alto Networks), FTNT (Fortinet), ZS (Zscaler), RBRK (Rubrik — data security and cyber resilience), PLTR (Palantir — AI/data platform for defence and intelligence), NET (Cloudflare — Zero Trust network security and SASE platform, native beneficiary of AI-era attack surface expansion; $85.5B FMP-verified), CYBR (CyberArk — Privileged Access Management and identity security, fastest-growing cybersecurity segment as AI agents multiply privileged credential attack surface; $20.6B FMP-verified), OKTA (Okta — Identity federation and access management; enterprise AI deployments multiply OAuth/SAML integrations and identity attack surface; direct beneficiary of AI-capex-driven security spend; identity attacks are the most common breach vector per IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2025). CIBR as First Trust NASDAQ Cybersecurity ETF proxy.

Mega-cap digital platforms with advertising leverage and content. GOOGL (Alphabet — search, cloud, YouTube), META (Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp ad platform), NFLX (streaming content, ad tier growing), DIS (Walt Disney — streaming Disney+/Hulu/ESPN+, fast-scaling ad-supported tier, strong content IP monetisation; $177.9B FMP-verified). XLC as Communication Services SPDR ETF proxy.

Consumer discretionary names benefiting from risk-on rotation and consumer confidence. AMZN (retail + AWS), HD (home improvement), MCD (resilient fast food), NKE (aspirational brand, consumer confidence proxy), LOW (Lowe's — direct complement to HD, same home improvement thesis; $120.1B FMP-verified), SBUX (Starbucks — consumer discretionary restaurant spend proxy, turnaround catalyst under CEO Brian Niccol; $113.5B FMP-verified). XLY as Consumer Discretionary SPDR ETF proxy. EAT (Brinker International — Chili's/Maggiano's; casual dining operator with high consumer spending leverage; benefits from risk-on rotation and real-wage growth; restaurant-specific alpha vs mega-cap retail names).

Travel and hospitality names for risk-on rotation. BKNG (Booking Holdings — online travel, strong FCF), MAR (Marriott — hotel operator, RevPAR leverage), DAL (Delta — airline, operational leverage), ABNB (Airbnb — platform model, asset-light), RCL (Royal Caribbean — cruise line, high leisure spend leverage, capacity expansion; $76.8B FMP-verified), HLT (Hilton — asset-light hotel operator, global RevPAR exposure; $74.9B FMP-verified). JETS as US Global Jets ETF proxy. Note: blocked if oil_supply_shock fires. H (Hyatt Hotels — luxury/upscale hotel operator, RevPAR leverage on business and leisure travel recovery; distinct from Marriott/Hilton on upscale mix and Asia-Pacific exposure).

Large-cap defensive healthcare for risk-off rotation. UNH (UnitedHealth — managed care, resilient earnings), LLY (Eli Lilly — GLP-1/obesity franchise, earnings momentum), ABBV (AbbVie — diversified pharma, dividend), TMO (Thermo Fisher — healthcare infrastructure, defensive revenue), MRK (Merck — diversified pharma, Keytruda oncology franchise, resilient earnings across cycles; $292.3B FMP-verified). XLV as Health Care SPDR ETF proxy. JNJ covered in defensive_quality. TFX (Teleflex — medical devices: catheters, vascular access, anesthesia; defensive recurring-revenue device maker; benefits from risk-off rotation into non-cyclical healthcare spend).

Large-cap biotech for risk-on environments. REGN (Regeneron), VRTX (Vertex — cystic fibrosis franchise), AMGN (Amgen — diversified large-cap biotech), GILD (Gilead Sciences — HIV/oncology franchise, resilient cash flows, risk-on biotech participation; $166.4B FMP-verified). XBI as SPDR S&P Biotech ETF proxy. Swing only — binary catalyst risk.

Regional bank names for credit easing and risk-on environments. RF (Regions Financial), CFG (Citizens Financial), FITB (Fifth Third Bancorp), MTB (M&T Bank — mid-Atlantic regional bank, strong commercial real estate and C&I lending book; $31.6B FMP-verified). KRE as SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF proxy. Swing only.

Industrial infrastructure and reshoring beneficiaries. CAT (Caterpillar — heavy machinery), DE (Deere — agriculture and construction equipment), GE (GE Aerospace — jet engines), URI (United Rentals — equipment rental, construction proxy), AMAT (Applied Materials — semiconductor fab equipment for US foundry buildout), CMI (Cummins — power generation and industrial engines, infrastructure buildout beneficiary; $89.5B FMP-verified), VMC (Vulcan Materials — crushed stone and aggregates, direct construction activity proxy; $36.8B FMP-verified). XLI as Industrial Select Sector SPDR ETF proxy. PCOR (Procore Technologies — construction project management software; infrastructure and reshoring capex drives SaaS subscription growth; direct beneficiary of US infrastructure bill spend and onshoring buildout).

Gold and precious metals for safe-haven bid and risk-off rotation. NEM (Newmont — largest gold miner), AEM (Agnico Eagle — diversified gold miner), WPM (Wheaton Precious Metals — streaming model, gold/silver royalties, asset-light exposure with leverage to precious metals prices; $60.1B FMP-verified). GLD (SPDR Gold Shares) as direct gold price proxy. GDX (VanEck Gold Miners ETF) as sector proxy.

Copper and electrification materials for risk-on and industrial demand. FCX (Freeport-McMoRan — largest US copper miner), SCCO (Southern Copper — pure-play copper with high dividend), BHP (BHP Group — world's largest diversified miner with major copper operations, US-listed ADR; $226.0B FMP-verified). XLB as Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF proxy.

REIT names for falling yield tailwind. PLD (Prologis — industrial REIT, logistics/e-commerce), AMT (American Tower — cell tower REIT), EQIX (Equinix — data centre REIT, AI infrastructure angle), DLR (Digital Realty Trust — data centre REIT, hyperscaler colocation and interconnection, direct AI data-centre real estate beneficiary; $67.0B FMP-verified). XLRE as Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF proxy.

Small-cap exposure for risk-on and credit-easing environments. IWM (iShares Russell 2000 ETF) and IJR (iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF) as liquid ETF proxies only. No individual small-cap selection — individual selection requires additional intelligence layer not yet built. Distinct from the small_caps headwind theme which fires under credit stress.

Building products and distribution names that directly benefit when yields fall and housing affordability improves. LPX (Louisiana-Pacific — structural panels and siding; housing starts leverage), FERG (Ferguson Enterprises — HVAC and plumbing distribution; largest US distributor, margin leverage on volume), CSL (Carlisle Companies — roofing systems and insulation; replacement and new construction demand). XHB as SPDR S&P Homebuilders ETF proxy. Activated by yields_falling driver.
